schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: THE OUTER-ROW PROJECTOR: the single owner-gated door that compiles the `intentions/` MODEL as a deterministic, lossless projection of its two source trees — `CairnCommons/intentions-other/` (the homeless intentions: the roots, concept-pieces, host-seams, spanning ones) PLUS the beside-code charters at `cairn/*/intention+why.json`. It reads both trees, embeds each source's content verbatim (a .json source as its parsed object, prose as text), orders the whole set by each source's BIRTH timestamp, and writes ONE artifact — `intentions/_model.json`. A pure core (`compile_model(sources)`) does the ordering + projection with no disk or git; a thin file layer (`gather_sources`, `_born`, `compile_to_disk`) is the today-shape. The single write-door is `compile_to_disk`; nothing else writes the compiled view, and the store's hand-authored `_charter+why.json` is never touched.
why: The seed claim of the `intentions/` store (its charter): take that ONE folder to a bare machine and regrow the system — which only works if the view compiles over BOTH sources, because the beside-code charters alone regrow a repo that does not RUN (everything that made a machine ready lives in `intentions-other/`). That demands a compiled view (Law 1 — the system-scale question 'what does this system intend' answered from structure, not re-derived by walking every file), written through one door (Law 6), that never drifts from its sources (Law 7 — the model is the view, the sources are the truth; a hand-edit is reverted on the next compile) and drops nothing (Law 8 — a lossless projection a hollow build could not fake). It is the OUTER row of the three-scale projector recursion (ticket: history→state; component: design→charter; SYSTEM: all sources→here) — the same move as `cairn/charter/projector.py`, one corpus out.
invoke: Hand-crank: `python3 -m cairn.intentions_model_compiler.compiler` (compiles the real repo → `intentions/_model.json`, prints the count + the roots that surfaced first). In code: `from cairn.intentions_model_compiler import compiler; compiler.compile_to_disk()` (roots auto-derived from `__file__`; override `commons_root`/`code_root`/`out_path` in proofs). Pure core, no disk: `compiler.compile_model(sources)` where each source is `{id, kind, source, born, content}`. Prove: `python3 cairn/intentions_model_compiler/proofs/test_compiler.py` (exit 0 = green).
node_class: code-seam — the compile-door child (a) of ticket intentions-model-compiler, now also carrying the WRITE GATE child (c): a code-seam script (recompile_gate.sh, git-tracked, proven) poked by a WRITE-THROUGH contract (the skills poke the gate in the same act that changes a source) plus a READ-REFRESH backstop (the reader pokes the gate before consulting the model); the prior FileChanged host hook is retired (superseded — see host_seam). Berths in cairn/ (class-space code); the folder it writes is CairnCommons/intentions/. Kin to cairn/charter (the projector), cross-referenced not merged.

host_seam.what: the MODEL-FRESHNESS CONTRACT — the sole write-door (compile_to_disk, via recompile_gate.sh) is poked by an EVENT, never by a clock or a resident watcher. Three invocations, no fourth, ALL event-driven, NO daemon: (1) MANUAL — the hand-crank / `cairn compile` (the human's command is the event); (2) WRITE-THROUGH baked into /sorted — the skill pokes the gate right after it files a homeless intention to intentions-other/ (the source-write is the event); (3) READ-REFRESH baked into /intent — the CONSUMER pokes the gate before consulting the model (the read is the event): at 'I intend X' the reader refreshes (a ~0.2s deterministic compile) then reads, catching every out-of-band write (a human hand-edit, a charter written outside a skill step) exactly when the model is needed and never on a no-op tick. The gate COMMAND (recompile_gate.sh) is class-space/git and proven; nothing stands running.
host_seam.why_not_incremental: MEASURED 2026-07-23 (Law 3): a full recompile of the whole corpus is 0.20s. So 'update vs rebuild' is a distinction without a difference — the writer pokes the ONE deterministic write-door for a full re-projection; there is NO incremental second write-path, which would break the single-door + determinism invariants (falsifiers 2 + 4). 'We don't rebuild by hand' is honored by the writer poking the cheap full door, not by partial updates.
host_seam.consumer: the model's live consumer now is CC + Akien reading it at 'I intend X' — start in the model: is anything already like this, or in conflict (Law 1, caught at n=1)? The check is JUDGMENT OVER THE WHYS the model carries, not a string-match — so no precomputed conflict-edges are needed and none are kept. The reader owns freshness by REFRESH-ON-READ — the callback-native backstop (the read is the event), restored after a wrong detour through a nightly cron daemon (drift; removed). Write-through keeps the model usually-already-fresh so the read rarely changes anything; the read-refresh guarantees no out-of-band write is ever silently stale, with nothing standing running.
host_seam.apply: the write-through call lives in /sorted and the read-refresh in /intent — both are skill instruction-steps, NO host config and NO daemon. Every trigger is an event (a command, a write, a read), never a clock. The freshness discipline is encoded in the skills, not left to CC to remember (Law 4).
host_seam.verify: after a /sorted that files to intentions-other/, confirm intentions/_model.json regenerated (mtime advanced); after a hand-edit to a source out-of-band, confirm the next /intent poke refreshes it before consulting. No host state to verify — there is no daemon and no crontab. The gate COMMAND itself is proven in git (proofs/test_recompile_gate.py).
host_seam.superseded: the FileChanged host hook (the prior child-c wiring) is RETIRED. MEASURED 2026-07-23 (Law 3): FileChanged did NOT fire on ANY write CC can produce — Write tool, Edit tool (post /hooks reload), or Bash subprocess — falsifying the provenance's birth-claim that it fires 'not only the agent's tool-writes'. It watches for changes from OUTSIDE CC's own operations, so it never caught the common case (CC authoring a charter during a build) and it dies with the CC session. The write-through-in-skills (the semantic writer pokes the door) is the resting mechanism it was standing in for; the wiring in .claude/settings.local.json is removed. The intentions-other/ heterogeneous-name problem that stumped the FileChanged matcher DISSOLVES here — /sorted pokes the door name-agnostically.
host_seam.iou: the freshness discipline now lives in the SKILLS, not in CC's memory (Law 4 — a rule that matters is structure, not policy that drifts): /intent refreshes-on-read, /sorted writes-through. The one remaining IOU is the owner-gate emit (the source's OWNER emitting on its own write — the emit-chokepoint state-machine-physics tracks); until it lands, the READ-REFRESH at /intent is the structural backstop that makes any missed eager write-through safe, needing no daemon because the read itself is the event. Named so the stopgap is not mistaken for the resting state.
host_seam.state: CONTRACT RECORDED + APPLIED 2026-07-23. Gate COMMAND proven + sealed (validations/test_recompile_gate.json). Write-through baked into /sorted; read-refresh baked into /intent — the freshness discipline lives in the skills (Law 4), not CC's memory. FileChanged wiring removed from .claude/settings.local.json. NO daemon: a nightly cron was installed then REMOVED as drift (a poll on a no-op tick; the read-refresh callback replaced it).

entry: FRESH build 2026-07-23, to the shape the `intentions/` store charter had already SPECIFIED as its unbuilt projector ('the projector for this scale does NOT exist yet ... a hand-written file here is caught by a reader, which is exactly the discipline-instead-of-physics that Cairn exists to stop relying on'). What is quarried: the projector's pure-core + thin-file-layer factoring and its atomic write-door. What is NEW: two-tree gathering (the seed claim), born-timestamp ordering so the roots surface first BY PHYSICS (Law 4, not a special case), and content-embedding so the one folder is self-contained.
state_and_history: compiled to state.json (projected window) + history.json (append-only voyage) beside this charter — the charter-state-history split; this file stays pure intention+why, the operational voyage lives in the sidecars (Law 1/4/7)
filed_edges: (a) the model is ONE _model.json embedding all content — grows to per-intention files or a richer index only if a real consumer needs it (build-minimal)

(b) the model is kept current by the MODEL-FRESHNESS CONTRACT (host_seam), ALL event-driven, NO daemon: MANUAL, WRITE-THROUGH baked into /sorted, READ-REFRESH baked into /intent; the prior FileChanged host hook is RETIRED
(c) Law-6 single-writer is an IOU enforced by convention until the tester import-scans for a second writer of intentions/
(d) born from git is the today-shape — swappable behind the pure core, which takes born as data
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_compiler.py, runnable bare (python3) and under the tester. Durability gate: not done until proven AND the VALIDATION is persisted beside-code (cairn/tester/validation_store.persist_validation → validations/<stem>.json) AND committed (git).
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) the model is not a faithful projection — a source is dropped or one is invented (Law 8), or content is mangled rather than carried verbatim; (2) the compile is non-deterministic or lossy (same sources → differing bytes — drift becomes invisible); (3) the roots do NOT surface first (born-ordering broken — the frame is not first by physics); (4) `intentions/_model.json` is written by any door other than `compile_to_disk`, or a hand-edit survives a compile, or the store's `_charter+why.json` is clobbered (Law 6/Law 7); (5) only one source tree compiles in (the seed claim broken — a bare-machine regrow would leave half the intent behind); (6) it grows its OWN authoring (content not traceable to a source — the view became a rival record of truth) or its own nightly scanner (that work is the inspector regime's, not this door's).
how_it_learns: Yes — it learns WHICH intentions are load-bearing by making the whole corpus legible in one ordered artifact, and it learns its own drift by construction: because the model is a pure function of the sources, any divergence between what the system SAYS it intends (the model) and what its sources hold is a compile away from being visible — the recompile IS the measurement. Its born-ordering is itself a learning stance: the frame surfaces first because it is oldest, so as the corpus grows the roots stay at the top without anyone maintaining that. Its horizon: whether one `_model.json` stays the right shape, or a real consumer (the web_server's intentions view, a bare-machine regrow) demands per-intention files or a queryable index — firmed up when that consumer arrives, not guessed now.
traces_to: Law 1 (the system-scale model is compiled once from structure, not re-derived by each reader — the outer row of the projector recursion)
Law 4 (roots surface first BY PHYSICS — born-timestamp ordering, not a hand-placed special case; the store charter's 'the_roots_surface_first')
Law 6 (exactly one owner gates writes to the compiled view — compile_to_disk is the sole write-door; IOU until the tester import-scans)
Law 7 (the compiled view is never a record of truth — a hand-edit is reverted; the sources are the truth)
Law 8 (a lossless projection a hollow build could not fake — nothing dropped, nothing invented)
Law 3 (the born source was chosen by MEASUREMENT — git --follow, after plain first-add was shown to sink the frame)
the `intentions/` store charter (CairnCommons/intentions/_charter+why.json — the seed claim + the assembly-point spec this builds to)
cairn/charter/projector.py (the kin — same pure-core + file-layer pattern, different corpus/owner; cross-referenced not merged)
ticket intentions-model-compiler (this is its child a — the compile door)
owner: CC owns the intentions_model_compiler code + its proof and gates writes to them. compile_to_disk is the sole owner-gated write-door for `intentions/_model.json`; per Law 6, a compiled record's own owner remains the owner of the SOURCE it came from — ownership travels with the source, not the projection.
proof: proofs/test_compiler.py — seven teeth a hollow compiler could not pass: lossless + invents-nothing; roots surface first by born timestamp (Law 4); a changed source changes the model; a removed source leaves no trace; deterministic to the byte; one write-door reverts a hand-edit AND spares the store charter (Law 6/7); gather reads BOTH source trees on the real repo and excludes the _-prefixed store charter (the seed claim). Green bare (python3) and under the tester; VALIDATION persisted beside-code at validations/test_compiler.json. The WRITE GATE child (c) carries its OWN proof — proofs/test_recompile_gate.py, three teeth a hollow gate could not pass: it pokes the door (the model appears from sources), it reverts a stale model, and it is non-blocking-but-loud-in-the-record (a failed compile exits 0 yet is logged, never swallowed — Law 7). Green bare and under the tester; VALIDATION at validations/test_recompile_gate.json.
provenance: Born 2026-07-23, built as child a of the intentions-model-compiler ticket cast the same session (from the /intent that bundled a command-runner, an intentions updater, and a 'repo-scanner' — the scanner resolving into the already-held inspector regime, not a child here). Built to a spec the `intentions/` store charter had written in advance ('the projector for this scale does NOT exist yet'). The real first compile surfaced a measurement finding — git first-add mis-dated the relocated roots — resolved to `git --follow` before the seal. The `cairn compile` CLI face (child b) landed the same day via cairn-command. The WRITE GATE (child c) followed 2026-07-23: cast in the primitives sweep that measured the mid-2026 CC feature landscape against Cairn — a FileChanged host hook (believed at birth to fire on EXTERNAL writes, catching a human hand-editing a charter, not only the agent's tool-writes — [CORRECTED 2026-07-23: MEASURED not to fire on ANY CC-produced write; RETIRED, superseded by the write-through contract — see host_seam.superseded]) built as a host-seam-with-IOU, Akien-ratified as the precedent-setting template for mounting borrowed event-primitives (Routines, hooks) behind Cairn's own doors. What that saga taught, folded into the resting design (2026-07-23): the trigger for 'a source changed' is the SEMANTIC WRITER (the skill) poking the door, not the host watching the filesystem; FileChanged is for changes from OUTSIDE CC's operations, which CC's own charter-writes are not. Still ahead: baking write-through into any remaining source-writers, and the owner-gate emit that retires the beside-code discipline-IOU (the read-refresh at /intent backstops it until then).
